DP-PostMerge: Free Compression via Post-hoc Merging
for Multi-Vector Visual Document Retrieval

Jianxin You, Kun Ni
Problem · Motivation · Method
Problem

Visual Document Retrieval (VDR). SOTA models like ColPali / ColQwen encode each page as ~700 patch vectors, then score queries via MaxSim late interaction:

Motivation

Two research camps disagree on how to compress:

Method
Main Finding — ViDoRe-V2 (4 datasets, ColQwen2.5)
Quantitative results : ViDoRe-V2
nDCG@5 vs compression on ViDoRe-V2 · avg of 4 datasets · 6 baselines + DP-PostMerge (★)
DP-PostMerge Pareto-dominates all baselines from 50% to 80% compression
k DocPruner
nDCG@5		 DocPruner
Compress.		 DP-PostMerge
nDCG@5		 DP-PostMerge
Compress.		 Free Δ
−0.50.598~39%0.598~52%+13%
−0.250.589~52%0.593~63%+10%
0.000.582~63%0.584~71%+8%
0.250.559~71%0.563~77%+7%
0.500.537~77%0.540~82%+5%

Same DocPruner k, ± post-hoc merge (θ = 0.93). PM gives +3–13% storage for free at matched (or better) quality.

Qualitative results : What Gets Merged
Example 1 — ESG report cover · 720 → 236 → 212 vectors · grey = pruned, color = merge group (★ anchor)
Example 2 — ESG appendix · 720 → 287 → 219 vectors · clusters: white tiles, red tiles, food tiles
What both examples reveal: the surviving "near-duplicate" groups are exactly the kinds of repeated content DocPruner can't distinguish — uniform backgrounds, repeated logos, structural padding, and tiled product images. Each absorbs 3–8 patches into one anchor vector with no measurable retrieval loss, which is the source of DP-PostMerge's 3–13% extra free compression in the table above.
Analysis & Generalization
Generalization — ViDoRe-V3
nDCG@5 vs compression ratio (avg across 7 ViDoRe-V3 datasets). Same pattern holds on a larger, more diverse benchmark.
Same gains on 7 new datasets with no hyperparameter search
θ Sensitivity
Avg across 4 ViDoRe-V2 datasets × 5 DocPruner k values. Δ nDCG = change vs DocPruner alone.
Runtime
Per-document latency on ColQwen2.5 (ViDoRe-V2, 1538 docs). VLM encoding dominates; merge adds only 19 ms (+2.5%).
Takeaways
  1. Importance ≠ redundancy. DocPruner is near-optimal for selecting patches, but leaves exploitable redundancy among survivors.
  2. Free compression. Post-hoc merge gives +3–10% extra compression at every operating point, with negligible quality loss and +2.5% runtime.
  3. Plug-and-play. Zero training, single θ, backbone-agnostic — attaches after any pruner as a drop-in second stage.
Setup: ColQwen2.5 · ViDoRe-V2 (4 ds) + V3 (7 ds) · nDCG@5 · k ∈ {−0.5,…,1.0} · θ = 0.93   |   Refs: DocPruner — Yan et al., 2025; ColPali — Faysse et al., 2024; ViDoRe — Macé et al., 2025   |   Code: github.com/jianxyou/project_6765
